name: Roy Cooper
subject: A code red for humanity.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
We are out of time. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34533088/302266171/823540626?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTExNzQzNi0zZTA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EreIFnG89O6HtjnMV7sITYdaEF0cvUpQrQLDNT45o5k=&emci=b0c45030-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=0a117436-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=1049176)
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34533091/302266173/55777720?source=em_RC_PET_Environment_082321_img&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTExNzQzNi0zZTA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EreIFnG89O6HtjnMV7sITYdaEF0cvUpQrQLDNT45o5k=&emci=b0c45030-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=0a117436-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=1049176) Peter, **“A code red for humanity.”** That’s the warning experts just issued in a U.N. report on the causes and consequences of climate change. **Simply put, we are running out of time.** _But there is still hope._ If we come together to take action now, we can mitigate the harm. We’ve already started here in North Carolina, where our administration has prioritized investing in green energy. We’ve developed wind and solar projects that will grow our economy while fighting climate change. And we’re getting a jump on boosting our economy with tens of thousands of new clean energy jobs. **[This report just proves that we can’t afford to prioritize politics over our environment for one second longer, so I’m calling on North Carolina Republicans to set aside partisanship and join us in supporting advancements in clean energy. Add your name right now to say you’re with me>>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34533093/302266176/55777720?source=em_RC_PET_Environment_082321_L1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTExNzQzNi0zZTA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EreIFnG89O6HtjnMV7sITYdaEF0cvUpQrQLDNT45o5k=&emci=b0c45030-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=0a117436-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=1049176)** Peter, it is our duty to future generations to leave them a planet that will sustain them. We’re custodians of this world, not the owners. Permanent climate change is now inevitable, but it’s still up to us to determine just how bad it will be. **If we’re going to save this planet for future generations, we need to take action at a societal level — right now.** That has to start by finding energy alternatives that don’t harm our environment, just as our administration is doing here in North Carolina. But we need the state’s Republican politicians to join us if we’re going to build the momentum we need to see to create a green energy future. **[Sign our petition to tell the GOP you support clean energy investments in North Carolina>>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34533095/302266177/55777720?source=em_RC_PET_Environment_082321_L2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTExNzQzNi0zZTA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EreIFnG89O6HtjnMV7sITYdaEF0cvUpQrQLDNT45o5k=&emci=b0c45030-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=0a117436-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=1049176)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34533096/302266178/55777720?source=em_RC_PET_Environment_082321_B1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwYTExNzQzNi0zZTA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=EreIFnG89O6HtjnMV7sITYdaEF0cvUpQrQLDNT45o5k=&emci=b0c45030-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&emdi=0a117436-3e04-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&ceid=1049176)

clean_body: We are out of time. | Peter, “A code red for humanity.” That’s the warning experts just issued in a U.N. report on the causes and consequences of climate change. Simply put, we are running out of time. But there is still hope. If we come together to take action now, we can mitigate the harm. We’ve already started here in North Carolina, where our administration has prioritized investing in green energy. We’ve developed wind and solar projects that will grow our economy while fighting climate change. And we’re getting a jump on boosting our economy with tens of thousands of new clean energy jobs. Peter, it is our duty to future generations to leave them a planet that will sustain them. We’re custodians of this world, not the owners. Permanent climate change is now inevitable, but it’s still up to us to determine just how bad it will be. If we’re going to save this planet for future generations, we need to take action at a societal level — right now. That has to start by finding energy alternatives that don’t harm our environment, just as our administration is doing here in North Carolina. But we need the state’s Republican politicians to join us if we’re going to build the momentum we need to see to create a green energy future. | Thanks, Roy = = = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper for North Carolina PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 United States Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. body:
Come see us on the Independence Mall where we have installed 1,700 flowers to honor the 1,700 Pennsylvanians who died from gun violence last year.
#

Friends,
[**The Gun Violence Memorial will be in Philadelphia from August 23-26 — and we’d love to see you there.**](https://go.giffords.org/go/2432?t=1&akid=725%2E3935704%2EOXUzxo)
We’ve installed 1,700 individual vases of flowers on the Independence Mall to honor the 1,700 Pennsylvanians who died needlessly from gun violence last year.
We encourage you to come visit the memorial in person between August 23-26 to honor these 1,700 victims, learn more about the fight to prevent gun violence, and urge your elected officials to take action. Click here for more information on how to get to the memorial:
**[VISIT THE MEMORIAL](https://go.giffords.org/go/2432?t=2&akid=725%2E3935704%2EOXUzxo)**
But if you can’t make it to the memorial in person, you can still participate by dedicating a flower to a victim of gun violence. Click the button below to dedicate a flower in honor of a victim or survivor of gun violence:
**[DEDICATE A FLOWER](https://go.giffords.org/signup/dedicate_a_flower_2021?refcode=em210823-1-15&t=3&akid=725%2E3935704%2EOXUzxo)**
No state or community in the United States is safe from gun violence. Nearly every American will know a victim of gun violence at some point in their lifetime. This memorial will visualize that reality in a bold and unique way that refuses to be overlooked or ignored.
We hope to see you soon,
Team Giffords

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=725%2E3935704%2EOXUzxo) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

name: =?utf-8?Q?Roger=20Marshall?=
subject: re: Afghanistan update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kansans for Marshall
body:
I supported President Trump’s decision and plan to withdraw from Afghanistan. But Joe Biden’s execution of this withdrawal has been a needless disaster and tragedy.

In recent days, many have asked where I stand when it comes to accepting refugees from Afghanistan. My answer is simple, and I wanted to make sure you saw it.

NO refugees until ALL Americans and people who worked and fought alongside us are safely evacuated.

And even then, we must ensure that all evacuees are properly vetted in another country to safeguard America’s national security. Period.

The Biden administration’s colossal failure has left thousands of Americans trapped behind Taliban lines and at risk of being stranded.

Getting them out must be our top priority.
Senator Roger Marshall

============================================================
Doc is defending life. He's standing for our jobs, our families, and our faith.

He's taking our Kansas values to the U.S. Senate, but he can't get it done without your help.

We know we send a lot of emails, but we're counting on you. The truth is, these emails are the key to keeping our supporters updated on ways they can make a difference.

However, if you'd like to receive fewer emails or unsubscribe, ** click here (https://kansansformarshall.us4.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=798e309beecf7512ab4f0f6d1&id=0799cdc67c&e=0b94e583bc&c=2b22147dab)
.

The message sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com by:
Kansans for Marshall
PO Box 1588
Great Bend, KS 67530
Paid for by Kansans for Marshall

clean_body: You won’t believe these numbers, Peter. | These polls are wild, Peter... In New Hampshire , the Democrats have a 4% lead... In Georgia , the Democrats are only up by 2%... In Florida , Republicans lead by just 2%... In the “Generic Ballot” , the race is effectively TIED This is going to be the closest election in our lifetime, Peter, and we need to be prepared for a long, hard, and expensive election battle. That’s why we need every Grassroots Republican to chip in at least $5 right away. The Democrats aren’t waiting! And we can’t afford to fall behind. | HELP SAVE AMERICA EMAIL: SUPPORTER ID: #05966106 DEADLINE: 11:59 PM TONIGHT SUGGESTED GIFT: $5 or more | | | | | | | Hurry, Peter! There’s no time to lose. body:
The Democrats worst nightmare is about to begin, and that starts with us. | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZyirkHyWd3RKHxUyb-t7vERxlO5YTNuYZ2l92oN4lh00Abj2s0mh6-Zq3_DQkhA4cQBVxlAsrjYs0dZos-qfVoaH-zWYbRLL-ki0Xvmx7oAkJWW75oHyQQvpM0eqQfhvQwgZVUnQw2HHxeXGfsaEPE6CDYr1kZHqRL0_t3o9YD9Og_WUfP9m1RtlVC4Re0Vcehw2Qt87chwTUm0PBZqkGIFc%3D)

---
Peter, let’s change the status quo.

For far too long Democrats have taken for granted minority voters, just expecting them to fall in line year after year.

Since I started my journey in public service, I have frustrated the radical Left. They hurl insults, and try to cancel me, but their standard 'anti-Republican' hating tactics will not work on me. I am a proud Afro-Latino, and I'm a Conservative.

I understand that 'tax and spend' bills hurt hard-working American families, small businesses, and even the national security of our country. I understand that what's happening at our border will NOT solve a long-broken immigration system. I understand that our individual rights are worth protecting.

[**Peter , this is why it is vital that we flip seats, regain the House Majority, and stop the socialization of our Democracy. **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZyiOq45fxtsAyezrnlDaBUzaj7nVlyPwUrN89YcVEKmnfnm597T7xOJYL22KQF4jb9eGbzsVPVsMuw9l5u0nw9dZUPKH3lLYEyKql3VjSa-lKSew4Eu-lgUkkX1rrddxgXQ9Snz584xrafdcOd2Vp_9D4bpg33fsTxqqOyxygjHbX_NJq3nCfq0zCjSq3TiPsM2Rcb4-I94fQC7g1vBiWY7o%3D)

| [Take Back the House ➔](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZynLM81BM2tVPL5u4LUmpE_Pk47A5dNjEZ7zJ_WoyKmN8vg4G4ZrXfUhfg45d7h1xPzvRjYdFmC8To9lS1BnzmhrurrkHnEqtvqey_rHySbVu6ifOXzJa0Q6bvT36a1xI6GzeJ9-xc9HwhSs9cofeQeyiJZ6jIlVwcGjOAgnUriOTRkRSSAerepRUSm6riIYVTNsptn1d5QbgGLswiv0VIuQ%3D)
---

Peter, I am a community leader. I am a mentor. I am a first generation African-American. I am an engineer working to make my state better.

I am ready to buckle down and start building a better nation for you and me, but before that can happen, I need your help. I'm asking - [**will you be able to pitch in $5 or $10 right now, so we can take back the House and get our government back in order?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZygHsPxKjb3EpeXx827RLuGZ3W8n20s43Ebx0SlrDEeEIVNZhGBNu8_IcB4Fr4F-OndkqDX3M3lm4jtr4cq_5GPW9SYd6ROAjSIYAk-32TKcWoQPO7hJlAozERq2eNLD_vagciwtjbpN69qkvGO3lwk2oraoiIaa73-vCxY4FNeSN7cTCBvX-g3cqCW95v6VoBChJNqGAlkIkOFoFKo02BG0%3D)

[Take Back the House ➔](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZypP6r3drK4a8fGkzdsv1ZHzO9BFt2e_QmjXYpxaJakbpjChHbliPBHvcb3YKQ5eUQm1ohHPjINKmyMZ0X9QySU__J_A98-1wyq_XRZ7gLa1AVMkM-5iNme-pEYUh3pD1oqR7DUHPqX18omNq3XHBXgmmKK0d_L86xht5tw3GXSkGb4Fya74b4ce2HdNuQlO7M_Zu5XEF8-hPqVt23Yt5VEc%3D)
---

There are going to be a million more hurdles to jump, Peter. But, with your support, I am confident that we will be successful and bring a voice for all conservatives!

Sincerely,

George S. Logan

P.S. D.C. might not be ready for this change, but I am. [**So , if you are too, please rush in a contribution of $5 or $10 today!**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=5136b2384c134b92970914fd3577a3b9&_e=VPs1W1U24YzN5IFcDdcZZQrTm2R_qps2jMFr6wwrD3TGSFoxXeAqNgODmcdk0S3d1nKAz2mifOKMauClBUiGSTYY0WXlQOAlIMtDAHVjCHLc94rYhXiPGxx1geeKjIrPvT2waHPGodFaPJThd5aW6OvAhE7ZBueh-24ek7zbHe7OijbZMEA8HpN2W6G7P_mDhjLyRWa8D1i_AyUZ5xeZygBFVLPbdW3cYLIUNMhRy6uSn_Zhh0jmdLRbVYfrGamw-HdPSA0ROWOyew7NFQOdRwgdzPjyaq3hF2nYsHaTaAu-X3UrhPRselQUVMaTbDLO9T9c9haECiuBk3-9lnLgmfu3iFcltRozuv7EubEVBinKs7CBeaid_1KBZhGoR1-knU7jsrZJPFL6b_YIYSXzRxWthWpR2Mdkx7ID7AmKR_4%3D)

clean_body: The Democrats worst nightmare is about to begin, and that starts with us. | Peter, let’s change the status quo. For far too long Democrats have taken for granted minority voters, just expecting them to fall in line year after year. Since I started my journey in public service, I have frustrated the radical Left. They hurl insults, and try to cancel me, but their standard 'anti-Republican' hating tactics will not work on me. I am a proud Afro-Latino, and I'm a Conservative. I understand that 'tax and spend' bills hurt hard-working American families, small businesses, and even the national security of our country. I understand that what's happening at our border will NOT solve a long-broken immigration system. I understand that our individual rights are worth protecting. | Peter, I am a community leader. I am a mentor. I am a first generation African-American. I am an engineer working to make my state better. I am ready to buckle down and start building a better nation for you and me, but before that can happen, I need your help. I'm asking - There are going to be a million more hurdles to jump, Peter. But, with your support, I am confident that we will be successful and bring a voice for all conservatives! Sincerely, George S. Logan P.S. D.C. might not be ready for this change, but I am. | | PAID FOR BY GEORGE LOGAN FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go, but .

Sharice has been a true ally to me in Congress since her first days in office. She’s been with me every step of the way as we fight for affordable health care and the protection of our public lands. Plus, as one of the first Native women elected to Congress, Sharice personally knows the importance of our tribal communities, which I’ve prioritized from the beginning of my career in public service.

[_**That’s why I’m asking you to give $20 today to support our work and keep us fighting for families in Arizona and Kansas.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34537928/302284947/-994249252?refcode=em_fr_2021.08.23_b1_TOH_all&amount=20&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODg2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNjk1ZDFiMS00ZDA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aXa1TV9Yn14fuWK8dcp3-D5ff1LAlxcuZxOoj8SAwBk=)

Thank you so much,

Tom O’Halleran
****_Tom O'Halleran has a proven track record of working across the aisle to get things done. He believes Congress has a responsibility to put aside partisan politics and work together to solve the problems affecting America and Arizona. With your help, we can find solutions to the problems that Arizonans and Americans face today._**

subject: This is a testament to what we’ve built together.

body:
Cheri has been working hard to earn support and build a grassroots coalition dedicated to flipping this Senate seat.

**Cheri has been working hard to earn support and build a grassroots coalition dedicated to flipping this Senate seat. We’ve got serious momentum because folks recognize that Cheri is the best candidate and gives Democrats’ the best chance to win.**

name: Senator Chris Van Hollen
subject: Senate Update: Moving Forward, Full Steam Ahead
body:
| | | [Click here to open this e-mail in its own browser window](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?link=SenVanHollen%2501153674%2501100546%2b-%2b08-23-2021%2beNewsletter%250115413.28652175.5403915.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501) | [Click here to open a plain text version of this email](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet//view_newsletter_setup.aspx?plain_text=Y&link=SenVanHollen%2501153674%2501100546%2b-%2b08-23-2021%2beNewsletter%250115413.28652175.5403915.7517223%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Friend%2501dpwillis67%2540gmail.com%2501Silver%2bSpring%252c%2bMD%2b20906%2501)
---|---
| | | **August 23, 2021** |
---|---|---|---
| | |
### Building Back _Better_
We have achieved a great deal since the start of this Congress - and I'm especially pleased with our work on getting our economy on the road to recovery. But as President Biden has underscored: it isn't enough for us to just "build back" from the pandemic - we need to Build Back Better, with an emphasis on "Better." That starts with growing our economy and creating jobs by fixing our crumbling roads and bridges, investing in transit systems across the country, modernizing our infrastructure to meet the challenges of the 21st century, and tackling the climate crisis. The [Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-senate-passage-of-the-bipartisan-infrastructure-bill&redir_log=277340132695913) my Senate colleagues and I passed earlier this month will do just that.

This bill includes major provisions of President Biden's American Jobs Plan, such as large investments to improve our transit systems, railways, clean water systems, roads, bridges, and tunnels - [bringing an estimated $6 billion for these projects alone to Maryland over the next five years](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.whitehouse.gov%2fwp-content%2fuploads%2f2021%2f08%2fMARYLAND_Infrastructure-Investment-and-Jobs-Act-State-Fact-Sheet.pdf&redir_log=357108981276828). Importantly, it also has critical investments to build the backbone for a more competitive, modern U.S. economy, including funds to expand broadband to end the digital divide and to start building out our clean energy grid and electric charging stations.

I worked to secure key elements in this plan to support Maryland directly, including investments in crucial economic drivers in our state like the Port of Baltimore and the Chesapeake Bay. I also fought to include [provisions I authored to reconnect communities](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-announce-inclusion-of-initiative-to-reconnect-communities-remove-harmful-infrastructure-like-baltimores-highway-to-nowhere-within-bipartisan-infrastructure-deal&redir_log=890897168751642) that had been split apart by 1960s federal transportation projects like the Highway to Nowhere in West Baltimore. And the bill includes a vital reauthorization of the $150 million annual federal contribution for WMATA for another eight years, policies I wrote to better protect our [frontline transit workers](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-napolitano-katko-introduce-legislation-to_protect-transit-workers-and-pedestrians-&redir_log=539637796981037), and [my bipartisan amendment to ensure taxpayer funded infrastructure projects are soundly financed](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fsenate-passes-van-hollen-amendment-to-bipartisan-infrastructure-deal-to-ensure-secure-financing-of-public-private-infrastructure-projects&redir_log=239600663991349). Crucially, we also succeeded in adding [language to keep federal funding for the Baltimore Red Line metro project alive](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-cardin-revive-federal-support-for-baltimores-red-line-transit-project&redir_log=270439820299940), despite the Governor's decision to pull the plug on this crucial investment. Finally, even before the pandemic began, I was working to connect every student, household, and business with affordable high-speed internet and expand the use of clean energy - and those investments in this bill, together with additional measures in the Democratic budget plan, will help keep Marylanders at the forefront of our global economy.

The Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill is an important step forward, but it is only a portion of the Build Back Better agenda. Right now, too many Americans are struggling to make ends meet - parents are juggling the costs of child care, millions of college graduates are drowning in student debt, seniors are weighed down by medical and prescription drug payments, and our workers and families are burdened by a tax system and an economy that doesn't work for them. That's why I've partnered with my colleagues on the Senate Budget Committee to assemble the [Build Back Better Budget](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-statement-on-senate-passage-of-the-build-back-better-budget-resolution&redir_log=609940537367942), which puts forward once-in-a-generation investments to change this.

Our budget plan puts everyday Americans first. It dramatically grows opportunity for all, cutting the costs of everything from prescription drugs to child care, expanding services covered by Medicare to include dental, hearing, and vision services for our seniors, and extending the new monthly tax cuts for families with children that we secured in the American Rescue Plan. As the impacts of climate change take a greater and greater toll on our daily lives, this budget turns a challenge into an opportunity, dedicating the necessary resources to begin tackling the climate crisis while creating millions of home-grown, good-paying, clean-energy jobs. This plan puts us on a path to a clean energy future, including through my proposal to create a national Clean Energy Technology Accelerator and my proposal to help Americans make their homes more energy efficient. Our framework also includes key provisions to create universal early education and make two-year community college free. Our Build Back Better Budget will raise the standard of living for workers, families, seniors, and students across Maryland and our country, and delivering these benefits to our state is a top priority. The budget plan, combined with the Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill, includes all key elements of President Biden's American Jobs Plan and American Families Plan - and more.

Our Build Back Better Budget will be paid for with long overdue reforms to our tax system to ensure that large corporations and the wealthiest of the wealthy pay their fair share to invest in the success of all. My colleagues and I have put together a menu of revenue options to pay for our budget - and Americans making under $400,000 a year won't pay an extra dime in taxes.

Our options begin with providing the IRS the necessary funds to enforce the current tax law, so that those cheating their fellow Americans - primarily some very wealthy individuals who don't report all their income - start paying what they already owe. The Treasury Department estimates that there were $600 billion in taxes that were owed but not paid in 2019 alone - and that the wealthiest of the wealthy are disproportionately to blame. As Chair of the Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Financial Services and General Government, I have already [held a public hearing](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.appropriations.senate.gov%2fhearings%2finternal-revenue-service-narrowing-the-tax-gap-and-improving-taxpayer-services&redir_log=34068992684887) with top IRS officials on this very issue, and I will continue pushing alongside colleagues to sharpen the IRS' enforcement capabilities. It's also vital that corporations start paying their fair share. I'm glad that President Biden has proposed returning the corporate tax rate to 28% and establishing a 21% minimum tax on the foreign profits of American corporations - along with undoing provisions from the 2017 Trump Tax Scam that encouraged corporations to [ship jobs overseas and park money in offshore tax havens](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-axne-introduce-bicameral-legislation-to-provide-transparency-on-corporate-use-of-tax-havens-offshoring-of-jobs&redir_log=082582862961226). I've also worked with my House colleague Representative Don Beyer to propose a [Millionaires Surtax](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-beyer-reintroduce-millionaires-surtax-to-invest-in-working-families&redir_log=674405369888929) that would apply an additional 10-percentage point tax to annual incomes above $2 million for married couples or above $1 million for individuals - and would apply equally to wages and salaries as well as to capital gains and other investment income - as the super-well-off get richer mostly by making money off of money rather than from their work. Our vision is straightforward, simple, and easy to implement, and this legislation wouldn't raise taxes for 99.8% of Americans. These are just a few of our many options going forward to ensure that the very wealthy and large corporations contribute more to invest in the success of every American. The huge income and wealth gaps in our country have grown even more in recent times. We must build a more inclusive economy with more shared prosperity.
---
### Making Polluters Pay
In addition to these ideas, earlier this month I put forward a plan that would help fund our proposals to tackle climate change in the Build Back Better Budget. This plan, which I announced with a number of my colleagues, requires the biggest emitters of greenhouse gasses doing business in the U.S. to pay into a [Polluters Pay Climate Fund](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-leads-senate-democrats-in-announcing-new-legislation-to-make-polluters-pay-for-climate-damage&redir_log=818434738871102) based on a percentage of their global emissions over the last two decades. The plan would impact about 25-30 of the biggest polluters - mostly big oil companies. The idea is patterned after Superfund, which was established to address damages from hazardous waste, and the monies collected would be used to finance a wide range of efforts to address the climate crisis - efforts that are at the very heart of our Build Back Better Budget.

For years, companies have made trillions in profits while spewing carbon pollution that wreaks havoc on our environment and harms public health. Now, every American is paying the price - from rising health costs to increasingly expensive climate mitigation efforts for everything from flooding to droughts to sea-level rise, and the U.N.'s Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has indicated that we're at a real tipping point and [must act now](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1424812479406874631&redir_log=00467911392374). Our idea is simple: big polluters should pay to help clean up the mess they caused - and those who polluted the most should pay the most. This proposal will ensure the costs of climate change are no longer borne solely by the American people - and instead require big corporate polluters to pay part of the clean-up bill.
### Taking Swift Action to Protect Americans and Our Afghan Allies
Like so many Americans, I have been distraught over the [terrible events unfolding in Afghanistan](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1426993403049521153&redir_log=190969878831373) and have heard from hundreds of Marylanders who are seeking assistance in helping friends and relatives evacuate. We are grateful to the brave Maryland service members who put their lives on the line and to their families who share in that sacrifice. There will be plenty of time to review our involvement in Afghanistan and how we reached this point, but now is not that time. Right now, we must be focused on getting Americans out of the country safely and evacuating the Afghans, and their families, who for over two decades worked directly with us to support our mission in that war-torn country. That means not only those who assisted our military, but also those who worked with the U.N. mission and with international NGOs that took on a large role in promoting literacy, women's education and empowerment, and other efforts to strengthen civil society. We cannot abandon these people. That cannot be our legacy. My staff and I have been working tirelessly with the State Department to advocate for the swift and safe evacuation of Americans and vulnerable Afghans, and I urge you to [contact](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fcontact&redir_log=475918421268285) my office if we can be of any assistance to you in this regard.

I recently joined 45 bipartisan Senate colleagues in urging the Biden Administration to take swift, robust action to protect and support Afghan women leaders facing unparalleled danger following the Taliban's violent sweep across Afghanistan and seizure of Kabul. In a [bipartisan letter](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.vanhollen.senate.gov%2fnews%2fpress-releases%2fvan-hollen-senate-colleagues-send-bipartisan-letter-urging-biden-admin-to-protect-afghan-women-leaders-in-wake-of-taliban-takeover-in-afghanistan&redir_log=359080469391386) to Secretary of State Antony Blinken and Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, my colleagues and I called on the Administration to create a humanitarian parole category specifically for women leaders, activists, human rights defenders, parliamentarians, journalists, and members of the Female Tactical Platoon of the Afghan Special Security Forces - and to streamline the paperwork process to facilitate referrals to allow for fast, humane, and efficient relocation to the United States.

I am continuing to monitor the situation in Afghanistan with my Senate colleagues, and we must do everything in our power to act decisively to get these people out and work with all parties who are able to assist in this effort in the critical days and weeks ahead. Moving forward, I will be working closely with my colleagues on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee and others to do a full review of U.S. policy towards Afghanistan over the years - including the final weeks of our engagement there. Chairman Menendez has already announced a hearing on this very issue, and it is imperative that the Senate faithfully fulfill its oversight role in this matter.
### Getting Around Maryland
Last week, I traveled across Maryland to mark the progress we've made this year with local stakeholders and community leaders - and discuss the hard work we still have ahead of us.

My trip across the state began last Monday, when I made a number of stops in Montgomery County, starting with a meeting with small businesses, theaters, and performance spaces that were hit hard by the pandemic and have benefitted from the new Shuttered Venue Operators Grant program that we fought to pass in Congress in December. I then traveled to the [Steven A. Cohen Military Family Clinic at Easterseals](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fposts%2f10158719956583878&redir_log=868366085288396), where I learned about the resources and services they provide to members of our Armed Forces and our veterans, who have made countless sacrifices for our nation. The clinic provides high-quality behavioral health care to veterans, active-duty service members, their families, and their caregivers - and during the visit I had the chance to thank the team for their hard work. I closed out the day by visiting the CareerCatchers offices in Silver Spring, Maryland, where I met with staff and volunteers to learn about the work they do to help provide vulnerable individuals with the training and resources they need to find and maintain employment.

On Tuesday, I traveled to the graduation ceremony of the [Chesapeake Conservation Corps Program](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fposts%2f10158719045733878&redir_log=875611776227395) - where I spoke with the 36 graduates of the program and welcomed the 33 newly-selected Corps members who will spend the next year working to improve the environment and local communities. The program was created by the late great Maryland State Senate President, my good friend Mike Miller, and it was inspiring to see his legacy live on through the new members of his beloved Corps. I also drove over to Annapolis and met with Anne Arundel County Executive Steuart Pittman and Annapolis Mayor Gavin Buckley [to learn about a new floodplain restoration project, tour the Annapolis City Dock, and discuss my new Polluters Pay Climate Fund proposal](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1428439436098945032&redir_log=150370839195374) and the need to take immediate action to tackle the climate crisis in order to protect Maryland's economy, small businesses, and families. I ended the day in Charles County at a [tri-county roundtable discussion](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2fposts%2f10158714819798878&redir_log=990554042797396) on bridging the digital divide, and I highlighted the broadband provisions we passed in the new Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill.

On Wednesday, I headed over to Baltimore City for a series of walking tours. I began the day touring the Belair-Edison neighborhood in Baltimore City with Safe Streets and Living Classrooms Foundation (LCF) staff and community members. Together, they're teaming up to create a new Crisis Management System and provide residents jobs to help grow opportunity in the area. Programs like these are key to creating greater prosperity, and I'm glad to see this initiative gain momentum. I then joined Councilmember Mark Conway, State Senator Mary Washington, Loyola University leadership, and community members for a walking tour of the York Road Corridor Initiative in the Govans neighborhood in Baltimore City. The York Road Initiative is a vital program to expand healthy food access for local residents, invest in long-term infrastructure improvements, and much more - and it was great to check out the extraordinary work they're putting into this key program. To support these efforts to strengthen our communities, I'm working to provide federal funding to back these crucial initiatives.

On Thursday, I headed to the Eastern Shore, where I delivered the keynote address at the Summer Conference Opening Session of the Maryland Association of Counties (MACo) and met with local lawmakers and county officials. In my remarks, I highlighted the extraordinary determination of our local leaders throughout the COVID-19 pandemic - and discussed the critical federal funding we secured for Maryland in the Senate through the CARES Act, the December COVID relief package, and the American Rescue Plan. Later that day, I joined my colleague, Senator Ben Cardin, in [Ocean City](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen%2fstatus%2f1429078532441915395&redir_log=082210214689374) to mark the start of the Ocean City Beach renourishment project led by the United States Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) and funded through federal dollars we fought to secure. That investment will help save the beach in Ocean City, which is an economic magnet that draws over 8 million visitors each year and supports hundreds of small businesses and thousands of jobs.

My week of traveling made two things clear: our state's economy is bouncing back from this crisis - and we couldn't have gotten where we are now without the determination, fortitude, and downright grit of Marylanders across our great state. We still have a long road ahead, and the next few months will determine whether we can truly Build Back Better. But I am confident that, together, we will succeed.

Chris Van Hollen
[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=Facebook&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.facebook.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=328155479639284 "Facebook")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=Twitter&redirect=https%3a%2f%2ftwitter.com%2fChrisVanHollen&redir_log=957640916473241 "Twitter")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=Instagram&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.instagram.com%2fchrisvanhollen%2f&redir_log=389295287187292 "Instagram")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=YouTube&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fwww.youtube.com%2fuser%2fRepChrisVanHollen&redir_log=463627374976326 "YouTube")[](https://outreach.senate.gov/iqextranet/iqClickTrk.aspx?&cid=SenVanHollen&crop=15413.28652175.5403915.7517223&report_id=&redirect=https%3a%2f%2fmedium.com%2f%40ChrisVanHollen&redir_log=575666539451239)

clean_body: | | | | ---|--- | | | August 23, 2021 | ---|---|---|--- | | | Building Back Better We have achieved a great deal since the start of this Congress - and I'm especially pleased with our work on getting our economy on the road to recovery. But as President Biden has underscored: it isn't enough for us to just "build back" from the pandemic - we need to Build Back Better, with an emphasis on "Better." That starts with growing our economy and creating jobs by fixing our crumbling roads and bridges, investing in transit systems across the country, modernizing our infrastructure to meet the challenges of the 21st century, and tackling the climate crisis. The my Senate colleagues and I passed earlier this month will do just that. This bill includes major provisions of President Biden's American Jobs Plan, such as large investments to improve our transit systems, railways, clean water systems, roads, bridges, and tunnels - . Importantly, it also has critical investments to build the backbone for a more competitive, modern U.S. economy, including funds to expand broadband to end the digital divide and to start building out our clean energy grid and electric charging stations. I worked to secure key elements in this plan to support Maryland directly, including investments in crucial economic drivers in our state like the Port of Baltimore and the Chesapeake Bay. I also fought to include that had been split apart by 1960s federal transportation projects like the Highway to Nowhere in West Baltimore. And the bill includes a vital reauthorization of the $150 million annual federal contribution for WMATA for another eight years, policies I wrote to better protect our , and . Crucially, we also succeeded in adding , despite the Governor's decision to pull the plug on this crucial investment. Finally, even before the pandemic began, I was working to connect every student, household, and business with affordable high-speed internet and expand the use of clean energy - and those investments in this bill, together with additional measures in the Democratic budget plan, will help keep Marylanders at the forefront of our global economy. The Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill is an important step forward, but it is only a portion of the Build Back Better agenda. Right now, too many Americans are struggling to make ends meet - parents are juggling the costs of child care, millions of college graduates are drowning in student debt, seniors are weighed down by medical and prescription drug payments, and our workers and families are burdened by a tax system and an economy that doesn't work for them. That's why I've partnered with my colleagues on the Senate Budget Committee to assemble the , which puts forward once-in-a-generation investments to change this. Our budget plan puts everyday Americans first. It dramatically grows opportunity for all, cutting the costs of everything from prescription drugs to child care, expanding services covered by Medicare to include dental, hearing, and vision services for our seniors, and extending the new monthly tax cuts for families with children that we secured in the American Rescue Plan. As the impacts of climate change take a greater and greater toll on our daily lives, this budget turns a challenge into an opportunity, dedicating the necessary resources to begin tackling the climate crisis while creating millions of home-grown, good-paying, clean-energy jobs. This plan puts us on a path to a clean energy future, including through my proposal to create a national Clean Energy Technology Accelerator and my proposal to help Americans make their homes more energy efficient. Our framework also includes key provisions to create universal early education and make two-year community college free. Our Build Back Better Budget will raise the standard of living for workers, families, seniors, and students across Maryland and our country, and delivering these benefits to our state is a top priority. The budget plan, combined with the Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill, includes all key elements of President Biden's American Jobs Plan and American Families Plan - and more. Our Build Back Better Budget will be paid for with long overdue reforms to our tax system to ensure that large corporations and the wealthiest of the wealthy pay their fair share to invest in the success of all. My colleagues and I have put together a menu of revenue options to pay for our budget - and Americans making under $400,000 a year won't pay an extra dime in taxes. Our options begin with providing the IRS the necessary funds to enforce the current tax law, so that those cheating their fellow Americans - primarily some very wealthy individuals who don't report all their income - start paying what they already owe. The Treasury Department estimates that there were $600 billion in taxes that were owed but not paid in 2019 alone - and that the wealthiest of the wealthy are disproportionately to blame. As Chair of the Senate Appropriations Subcommittee on Financial Services and General Government, I have already with top IRS officials on this very issue, and I will continue pushing alongside colleagues to sharpen the IRS' enforcement capabilities. It's also vital that corporations start paying their fair share. I'm glad that President Biden has proposed returning the corporate tax rate to 28% and establishing a 21% minimum tax on the foreign profits of American corporations - along with undoing provisions from the 2017 Trump Tax Scam that encouraged corporations to . I've also worked with my House colleague Representative Don Beyer to propose a that would apply an additional 10-percentage point tax to annual incomes above $2 million for married couples or above $1 million for individuals - and would apply equally to wages and salaries as well as to capital gains and other investment income - as the super-well-off get richer mostly by making money off of money rather than from their work. Our vision is straightforward, simple, and easy to implement, and this legislation wouldn't raise taxes for 99.8% of Americans. These are just a few of our many options going forward to ensure that the very wealthy and large corporations contribute more to invest in the success of every American. The huge income and wealth gaps in our country have grown even more in recent times. We must build a more inclusive economy with more shared prosperity. Making Polluters Pay In addition to these ideas, earlier this month I put forward a plan that would help fund our proposals to tackle climate change in the Build Back Better Budget. This plan, which I announced with a number of my colleagues, requires the biggest emitters of greenhouse gasses doing business in the U.S. to pay into a based on a percentage of their global emissions over the last two decades. The plan would impact about 25-30 of the biggest polluters - mostly big oil companies. The idea is patterned after Superfund, which was established to address damages from hazardous waste, and the monies collected would be used to finance a wide range of efforts to address the climate crisis - efforts that are at the very heart of our Build Back Better Budget. For years, companies have made trillions in profits while spewing carbon pollution that wreaks havoc on our environment and harms public health. Now, every American is paying the price - from rising health costs to increasingly expensive climate mitigation efforts for everything from flooding to droughts to sea-level rise, and the U.N.'s Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change has indicated that we're at a real tipping point and . Our idea is simple: big polluters should pay to help clean up the mess they caused - and those who polluted the most should pay the most. This proposal will ensure the costs of climate change are no longer borne solely by the American people - and instead require big corporate polluters to pay part of the clean-up bill. Taking Swift Action to Protect Americans and Our Afghan Allies Like so many Americans, I have been distraught over the and have heard from hundreds of Marylanders who are seeking assistance in helping friends and relatives evacuate. We are grateful to the brave Maryland service members who put their lives on the line and to their families who share in that sacrifice. There will be plenty of time to review our involvement in Afghanistan and how we reached this point, but now is not that time. Right now, we must be focused on getting Americans out of the country safely and evacuating the Afghans, and their families, who for over two decades worked directly with us to support our mission in that war-torn country. That means not only those who assisted our military, but also those who worked with the U.N. mission and with international NGOs that took on a large role in promoting literacy, women's education and empowerment, and other efforts to strengthen civil society. We cannot abandon these people. That cannot be our legacy. My staff and I have been working tirelessly with the State Department to advocate for the swift and safe evacuation of Americans and vulnerable Afghans, and I urge you to my office if we can be of any assistance to you in this regard. I recently joined 45 bipartisan Senate colleagues in urging the Biden Administration to take swift, robust action to protect and support Afghan women leaders facing unparalleled danger following the Taliban's violent sweep across Afghanistan and seizure of Kabul. In a to Secretary of State Antony Blinken and Secretary of Homeland Security Alejandro Mayorkas, my colleagues and I called on the Administration to create a humanitarian parole category specifically for women leaders, activists, human rights defenders, parliamentarians, journalists, and members of the Female Tactical Platoon of the Afghan Special Security Forces - and to streamline the paperwork process to facilitate referrals to allow for fast, humane, and efficient relocation to the United States. I am continuing to monitor the situation in Afghanistan with my Senate colleagues, and we must do everything in our power to act decisively to get these people out and work with all parties who are able to assist in this effort in the critical days and weeks ahead. Moving forward, I will be working closely with my colleagues on the Senate Foreign Relations Committee and others to do a full review of U.S. policy towards Afghanistan over the years - including the final weeks of our engagement there. Chairman Menendez has already announced a hearing on this very issue, and it is imperative that the Senate faithfully fulfill its oversight role in this matter. Getting Around Maryland Last week, I traveled across Maryland to mark the progress we've made this year with local stakeholders and community leaders - and discuss the hard work we still have ahead of us. My trip across the state began last Monday, when I made a number of stops in Montgomery County, starting with a meeting with small businesses, theaters, and performance spaces that were hit hard by the pandemic and have benefitted from the new Shuttered Venue Operators Grant program that we fought to pass in Congress in December. I then traveled to the , where I learned about the resources and services they provide to members of our Armed Forces and our veterans, who have made countless sacrifices for our nation. The clinic provides high-quality behavioral health care to veterans, active-duty service members, their families, and their caregivers - and during the visit I had the chance to thank the team for their hard work. I closed out the day by visiting the CareerCatchers offices in Silver Spring, Maryland, where I met with staff and volunteers to learn about the work they do to help provide vulnerable individuals with the training and resources they need to find and maintain employment. On Tuesday, I traveled to the graduation ceremony of the - where I spoke with the 36 graduates of the program and welcomed the 33 newly-selected Corps members who will spend the next year working to improve the environment and local communities. The program was created by the late great Maryland State Senate President, my good friend Mike Miller, and it was inspiring to see his legacy live on through the new members of his beloved Corps. I also drove over to Annapolis and met with Anne Arundel County Executive Steuart Pittman and Annapolis Mayor Gavin Buckley and the need to take immediate action to tackle the climate crisis in order to protect Maryland's economy, small businesses, and families. I ended the day in Charles County at a on bridging the digital divide, and I highlighted the broadband provisions we passed in the new Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill. On Wednesday, I headed over to Baltimore City for a series of walking tours. I began the day touring the Belair-Edison neighborhood in Baltimore City with Safe Streets and Living Classrooms Foundation (LCF) staff and community members. Together, they're teaming up to create a new Crisis Management System and provide residents jobs to help grow opportunity in the area. Programs like these are key to creating greater prosperity, and I'm glad to see this initiative gain momentum. I then joined Councilmember Mark Conway, State Senator Mary Washington, Loyola University leadership, and community members for a walking tour of the York Road Corridor Initiative in the Govans neighborhood in Baltimore City. The York Road Initiative is a vital program to expand healthy food access for local residents, invest in long-term infrastructure improvements, and much more - and it was great to check out the extraordinary work they're putting into this key program. To support these efforts to strengthen our communities, I'm working to provide federal funding to back these crucial initiatives. On Thursday, I headed to the Eastern Shore, where I delivered the keynote address at the Summer Conference Opening Session of the Maryland Association of Counties (MACo) and met with local lawmakers and county officials. In my remarks, I highlighted the extraordinary determination of our local leaders throughout the COVID-19 pandemic - and discussed the critical federal funding we secured for Maryland in the Senate through the CARES Act, the December COVID relief package, and the American Rescue Plan. Later that day, I joined my colleague, Senator Ben Cardin, in to mark the start of the Ocean City Beach renourishment project led by the United States Army Corps of Engineers (USACE) and funded through federal dollars we fought to secure. That investment will help save the beach in Ocean City, which is an economic magnet that draws over 8 million visitors each year and supports hundreds of small businesses and thousands of jobs. My week of traveling made two things clear: our state's economy is bouncing back from this crisis - and we couldn't have gotten where we are now without the determination, fortitude, and downright grit of Marylanders across our great state. We still have a long road ahead, and the next few months will determine whether we can truly Build Back Better. But I am confident that, together, we will succeed. name: Derek Marshall
subject: This election denier needs to go.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Derek Marshall for Congress
body:
Peter,
**I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: this campaign is not about me, or my ego, or my desire to become a career politician.** This campaign is about a movement of people usually left behind in politics coming together to make a much-needed change in our community. What change, exactly?
Let me clear. **I don’t believe that the Trump-backed Republican incumbent Jay Obernolte really represents the people of California’s 8th district.** He certainly doesn’t represent me. That’s exactly why I launched this campaign for Congress almost two years before Election Day. I know it will be an uphill battle to flip this red district blue, but I believe that there is a better future for my home district and I was ready to invest all my time and faith in it.

[**If you think we need a change in Congress too, you can help RIGHT NOW by donating to our movement. **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34538891/302288539/-996156963?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjg1NTJlOC01MDA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GfEcb4XeTQ7OOaE1W-3pPzbkSYNb4wqZqHddpjMoaHA=)

**Obernolte cast one of his first votes against certifying the presidential election results.** That’s right, he denies the free and fair election we had, which is a cornerstone of American democracy. He believes that “radical policies” like common-sense police reform are “making our communities less safe.” He even voted against the Equality Act, because he doesn’t think that people like me deserve equal opportunity and protection.

**As a proud, progressive gay man, I can say with absolute confidence that this district deserves better.** My team and I know that there are tens of thousands of unregistered voters in this district that are waiting to be engaged by campaigns instead of left out. There are thousands of disengaged, disenchanted voters who need something real to believe in again. And we’ve made it our mission to reach all of them.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34538892/302288540/-996156963?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjg1NTJlOC01MDA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GfEcb4XeTQ7OOaE1W-3pPzbkSYNb4wqZqHddpjMoaHA=)

That’s why your investment is so important. Every dollar we raise allows us to invest in the staff and the tools we need to revive our electorate. This people-powered movement is growing stronger by the day thanks to your support. **[If you can donate today, we’ll be in a better position to defeat this Trump-backed opponent and take his seat in Congress.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34538893/302288541/-996156963?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84ODEyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlYjg1NTJlOC01MDA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=GfEcb4XeTQ7OOaE1W-3pPzbkSYNb4wqZqHddpjMoaHA=)**

**Derek Marshall**

clean_body: Peter, I’ve said it before and I’ll say it again: this campaign is not about me, or my ego, or my desire to become a career politician. This campaign is about a movement of people usually left behind in politics coming together to make a much-needed change in our community. What change, exactly? Let me clear. I don’t believe that the Trump-backed Republican incumbent Jay Obernolte really represents the people of California’s 8th district. He certainly doesn’t represent me. That’s exactly why I launched this campaign for Congress almost two years before Election Day. I know it will be an uphill battle to flip this red district blue, but I believe that there is a better future for my home district and I was ready to invest all my time and faith in it. = Obernolte cast one of his first votes against certifying the presidential election results. name: When Democrats Turn Out
subject: Gov. Kemp has blood on his hands
disclaimer_text: Paid for by When Democrats Turn Out PAC
body:
|
---

| | Peter, COVID-19 has created a political situation where every decision your governor makes will do one of two things \-- save lives or cost lives. Peter, the sad truth is: Republican Governor Brian Kemp has made a series of decisions that have resulted in tens of thousands of heartbreaking, unnecessary deaths in Georgia. From day one of lockdown, Kemp has been dead-set on reopening his state no matter the human cost. Now Kemp is pretending that the surging, lethal Delta variant doesn't exist. Kemp won't enforce a mask mandate, expand healthcare, or make sure vulnerable communities have access to vaccines. One exasperated progressive critic put it best: “He's just throwing up his hands like he has no power to save lives.” **There's only one way to end the GOP's funeral march: Her name is Stacey Abrams.** Stacey Abrams is ready to lead Georgia out of this pandemic with dignity. Georgians deserve a leader who will put their lives ahead of any reckless partisan agenda. As progressives and as moral agents, we have a responsibility to do all we can to help Stacey end Kemp's blood-soaked career. We are doing all we can to help elect candidates that will stand with Stacey Abrams, demand that every vote is counted, continue fighting even when things get tough, and refuse to get knocked down, even in the face of GOP voter suppression. But we're falling short of our fundraising goal and are asking for your urgent support immediately. **[This can't wait, Peter: Take a moment right now to rush us $5, $10, $25, or even more, and help us support every candidate who will stand with Stacey Abrams in her fight against GOP voter suppression. name: CNN BREAKING UPDATE (via DCCC)
subject: SHOCKING Senate update
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DCCC | 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington, DC 20003 | [(202) 863-1500](http://dccc
body:
| | | _**CNN BREAKING: The Senate playing field just tipped toward Republicans **_
We know Speaker Pelosi reached out yesterday about the fight for the Senate, but we wanted to make sure you saw the absolute latest.

CNN just released a SHOCKING report that our Senate Majority is in deep trouble -- and now we're closer than ever before to losing it all.

**Hear us: We refuse to let Trump and his handful of fringe Republicans destroy our Congressional Majorities and push their far-right agenda onto the American people. We must stop them from destroying our House Majority.**

But we can't win this fight alone.

_**NOT** while they're launching attacks on our most vulnerable Democrats. _

_**NOT** while they're shattering fundraising records left and right._

_**And NOT** while they're undermining voting rights and preparing to gerrymander their way back into power._

**[That's why we're counting on our very best grassroots supporters --_ like you_ \-- to chip in $3 to DEFEND our House Democrats and stop Republicans from DESTROYING our Democratic Trifecta.](https://links.e.dccc.)**

We wouldn't ask if it weren't dire \-- so please, can we count on you?__

name: NinaTurner.com
subject: Can you please split a $10 contribution between the Haiti Emergency Relief Fund and the National Haitian Elected Officials Network?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nina Turner for Us
body:
When it comes to the climate crisis, we are all in this together.

We'll get right to it — we are writing to ask you to split a $10 contribution between two organizations working on the ground to bring relief to the people of Haiti after the devastating earthquake last week.
[**Can you please split a $10 contribution between the Haiti Emergency Relief Fund and the National Haitian Elected Officials Network?**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34539284/302289523/-1208277935?refcode2=3f5b0d38-5204-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&refcode=em210823-1-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjViMGQzOC01MjA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bhnnNED0DS-IoAe7z9VfBHNgFFpjJ24PjBIreU1vz5E=)
**[STAND WITH HAITI](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34539285/302289524/-1208277935?refcode2=3f5b0d38-5204-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&refcode=em210823-1-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjViMGQzOC01MjA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bhnnNED0DS-IoAe7z9VfBHNgFFpjJ24PjBIreU1vz5E=)**
The sad truth is that escalating climate chaos means that this is not the first natural disaster that the people of Haiti have had to face, and it certainly won’t be the last. That’s why it’s so important we support the people of Haiti as they begin the hard work of recovering from the recent earthquake.
And what the people of Haiti have said repeatedly is that they do not want outside groups that come in and profit off of disasters — what they need from folks like us is to support them directly.
So that’s what we’re asking you to do today:
By contributing to the **Haiti Emergency Relief Fund** , your donations will go directly to grassroots organizations in Haiti that are saving lives by helping people on the ground access water, food and shelter, and reconstructing their lives. HERF is volunteer-led, meaning 100% of your contribution goes to the people who need it most.
**National Haitian Elected Officials Network (NHAEON)** partners with the Haitian Government, USAID, and community organizations to rapidly provide much-needed resources to Haitian families who have been directly impacted by the earthquake. By donating today, 100% of your donation will go towards supporting the efforts of trusted organizations on the ground in Haiti.
[**If you can afford it, please split a donation of $10 — or whatever you can afford to give right now — between these two organizations helping people in need.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34539286/302289525/-1208277935?refcode2=3f5b0d38-5204-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&refcode=em210823-1-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjViMGQzOC01MjA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bhnnNED0DS-IoAe7z9VfBHNgFFpjJ24PjBIreU1vz5E=)
Thank you for standing with the people of Haiti. And remember, when it comes to the climate crisis, we are all in this together. If you are in the position to donate, please consider making a contribution today.
With these hands,
Team Nina Turner
**[STAND WITH HAITI](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/34539287/302289526/-1208277935?refcode2=3f5b0d38-5204-ec11-b563-501ac57b8fa7&refcode=em210823-1-b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzZjViMGQzOC01MjA0LWVjMTEtYjU2My01MDFhYzU3YjhmYTciLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=bhnnNED0DS-IoAe7z9VfBHNgFFpjJ24PjBIreU1vz5E=)**

body:
A terrorist emerged from the bathroom, bare-chested and armed with an assault rifle, a jar of gasoline, and 270 rounds of ammunition attempted to take I stopped a terrortist attack on a train to Paris, now I'm ready to serve the great state of Oregon in Congress. | [](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEooTon_mI3pU_MOGChsPVKB2PpyIQVhTtPs4fydYSVZ6D937gJzfYWsDkBAXyFT6ho4zo7SCW5ZyTqsnSWEGjQYSDlbZohFqI7wKoLuA9ARmcEq_JhW25Ssw7nz_NwWZpyiRL8j0l8g4kS9i_iaQ-RyPpc1jb8SbItp4ulsQwSaA2hD3micLw1bAjufKC_iQDXLaUqheuiWr7ZvxS8zqIeAw%3D%3D)

Peter,

Like so many Americans, I am watching the scenes coming out of Afghanistan with disbelief, extreme sadness and even anger. You see, I served the greatest country on Earth in Afghanistan back in 2015. But Afghanistan wasn't the only place I looked terrorism directly in the eyes...

**August 21 st marked six years since the fateful late-summer afternoon that changed my life and those of my two best friends - forever.**
[](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEo21Myuw-3azOrhN4HZSppvMB8a-KWC1TGAiic-g5ODf9HSory73gWTbEyDrZdK2EmDnc8mbHV0sdXH_woU1KTW6xlLjfa_bj_nFrw1bxkfhIilTgObLgXroe2_qv_oAQn8kBAOGn1rtGmAm_2qKdaFJ4d8TEunLF6YByN55Pqt8IMlCgb2eFPt4wDKgugGjez-ND7ZOdYuuRcy5uriW8usw%3D%3D) I had just finished serving our great nation in Afghanistan when my best friends and I decided to take a vacation in Europe. We were traveling on a Paris-bound train when a terrorist emerged from the bathroom, bare-chested and armed with an assault rifle, a jar of gasoline, and 270 rounds of ammunition attempted to take over and kill us all.

As a soldier, I was trained to take out a terrorist threat -As an American, I was taught to stand up for freedom no matter where I was in the world. **Which is why I, my friends and other brave travelers took the action needed to stop this terrorist.**

This split-second decision prevented what witnesses contend could have become a mass slaughter. There is a reason I was able to make this split-second decision to intervene, Peter - **I have and always will be willing to give my life in the fight for freedom, in the image of true American values.**

[**At the end of this email, I am going to ask you to donate $6 to my campaign for Congress, in honor of the sixth anniversary of the Thalys train attack. But first, you need to understand why we need an American fighter like myself in Washington.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEoOec80YbW2zd0vS8J0I7_s9o6yHAbIl0oHn_riykm2AXUVsWHGIiaYIYr7S8soHESLGoVB4h2uT8ZI02KrNIBU2dNsZSmmpLZeXkbYiYDvaPk_jopO_URGn32GzQ8s-af4vpfqAxKFa4qw5k87E7L8JhbctzvI_m5oWJu5Y_4RjDobchJXXgzmYDRBbskG3q0UOZw87ftYTs5lL_FTc6XFA%3D%3D)

[**DONATE $6 TODAY -> **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEorr85VBQkiy7gNeoNRhHtFF0YMaO5h6M1payVW_F3Stuga1ZwR4BpFPQqjAQ48zdc-xsLoQDjwi3j6l-53O-0uPMvlT1BIzkUtVG2_20XKu2JhMUxM8gni0jnEtkIoC1QL4oWUAHo4q7kLY8lOZ0srYQq3FEq-AAjs5fO5sA2btmSJNAsjDQv2wBnYh-kxrR85eQQXHg9-6OIuWGfq30xKg%3D%3D)
Now, our homeland is in danger. Not from radical Islamic terrorists, but radical Democrats who want to fundamentally alter the fabric of this GREAT nation. Leftist legislation is assaulting our Nation, our communities, and the American Dream.

Right now, Democrats are...
* Opening and destroying our borders. They are g**ranting dangerous amnesty** that will flood every corner of America.
* **Raising our taxes** like we have never seen, and making common goods like gas and groceries unaffordable.
* **Defunding the police,** leading to a surge in violent crime
* **Erasing historical figures** from our schools like George Washington and Abraham Lincoln
* Teetering on the edge of a **dangerous foreign policy crisis**

These power-hungry Democrat elitists like Nancy Pelosi and Joe Biden don’t care about the people they represent. **They only care about their own radical agenda, and my opponent, Peter DeFazio is one of the primary offenders.**

The Biden Democrats' grip on power in Washington is seriously undermining our freedom, prosperity, and security. The only way to pull America out of this leftist tailspin is by taking back the House in 2022. **We need a fighter in Congress, Peter and I hope that hearing my story proves to you that I am the man for the job.**

My life’s mission has always been to represent the America I know and love - the world’s bastion of freedom and economic prosperity. [**I am taking that mission and putting it into action by running for Congress, by fighting for my neighbors and their wellbeing in Washington.**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEoScs4id6Rvy-ifNADtCndnxBnsNsgc86YNRcMjAVCpYiJle79n6uch_u7trKeop4ixWy6EaNblTXmTrGjwF_U4igrRaccfk4gUT1ua7Gd2mopNsGIrHpEGrCzqJyptXT0y1PEpNUq6FmCcsPErBRCwAMvVSvs3cMBKtDkLh5MCDLoBymfdZ4zJJmA_Wai65Nrcdyqzq59NXwnR-Zw-QYmbw%3D%3D)

This will not be an easy road, which is why I am reaching out to you. I will need the financial support of every American patriot; every single dollar will help. [**Can I count on you to join my team with a donation of $6 - to mark my sixth anniversary of thwarting a terrorist attack?**](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEoEbHrtkwKKHaWfEtwo0tsPcbzvXaqeBRGu8W_SirOJbZO-B5Wiw2HGXErP9rwD7wkSzHWdIR2s6Q6BfPH2j9psjCIsHg8XA9BN7HQ__Mb4gktFiXSTUMPymcA5dckxijNU4tgtb6hATi9fa6ZwuggeA5r0569u5xIA47dUck-JpyMSwzL0LAj2EAFuGqZh5riyB6xbda9Z0-Ibq3BXBQhLQ%3D%3D)

[**DONATE $6 TODAY -> **](http://links.bestamericanow.com/u/click?_t=f6e78b69acbb4291bd75bc93fae35a62&_m=ab3c2fa67dc645e5a1580c9bdf6c9f49&_e=57vwZXnKblzQHgl_4fQE-7E0ESm-rQETXvBINezV2XwOW72xI5JqP4M452DLm0ZMJlr6LSayqfRgkE8Ebu_ROBL5Tbb53iR46rY-ukR4Sa7xWp8obuZueyVA0mXbAdeoOoWDKZDcgyl6Agr7BQ6Soz5bRApiIjkxHE2HwmPxQza4ooUTDUP6r3BhNFu2bTufcumH_kE1ZvmLPtKirMmB7v4Th5NyUUbq_Qzyq_ZifXzOqDiCHQf7Mpyzib0ydtEozmOZ33Hh8Sin9oLK9mx63MYUmtCtD5D4I25FsQqq_tkQ0di2w-_VcTvsLk0CbYvT0Jr4sD8JdggS7WrNp1pVfgIaMyPMYxVg3tz6jIbOQrHmXLdQB_2Ygreap0ND9qHAmvPYdvYW-jnI2d85KL_IykHvdwwTofklbkS6Bypybs5RJSpqUYUAyzqMMxuWX5fUVpezH5kWlkC-xwSUcBgZtA%3D%3D)

Thank you very much,
Alek Skarlatos
Republican for Congress

| Alek is a former Oregon National Guardsman, completing a nine-month deployment in Afghanistan. Alek, along with four others, stopped an armed terrorist on a Paris-bound train traveling from Amsterdam to Paris in August 2015. His heroism earned him several awards and medals around the world.
